title: Verify Property Response
description: The brand-agent's authoritative answer on property ownership. Mirrors the property-relationship enum used in brand.json properties[].

status.description: Verification status. Subset of /schemas/brand/verification-status.json applicable to property claims. `pending_review` is excluded because property ownership is generally definitive — agents that need to surface in-process state SHOULD use `transferring`. `licensed_in` / `licensed_out` are excluded because licensing applies to trademarks and brands, not the underlying digital property.

relationship.description: Public — when status is `owned` or `transferring`, the commercial relationship between the brand and the property. Mirrors brand.json's properties[].relationship enum. Omitted when status is not in that set.

brand_id.description: Public — the brand_id within the house this property belongs to. Lets the caller cross-reference with brand.json data.

regions.description: Public — ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes where this property is the brand's primary/canonical surface. Use the sentinel `"global"` (matching the request schema's region field) to signal no regional restriction. Omitting the field is equivalent to `unknown regions`.

use_case_authorization.description: Authorized-tier only. Per-use-case authorization flags. The agent's answer to whether the named use case is permitted on this property — distinct from ownership. Registered keys: advertising, endorsement, retail_listing, editorial, commercial_advertising, merchandise_resale. Agents MAY add additional keys (additionalProperties: boolean).

endorsement.description: Use of the property in endorsement deals.

merchandise_resale.description: Resale of merchandise associated with the property.

context_note.description: Public — free-text context the brand chooses to surface (e.g., 'Regional CN site', 'Legacy domain, redirects to nike.com'). When status is `disputed`, carries the rationale.
